MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. GREGORY A. CLARICK, ESQ. of Clarick Gueron Reisbaum (Tel No. 212-633-4313), and JAMIE L. DUPREE, ESQ. of Futterman Dupree Dodd Croley Maier LLP (Tel No. 415-399-3840) appearing for Defendant Raymond Pettibon;
Atty. ERIC MCDONOUGH, ESQ. of Seyfarth Shaw LLP, Tel No. 213-270-9676 appearing for Defendant Christian Strike.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT RAYMOND PETTIBON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Mr. Pettibon has not satisfied his initial burden of production on any of the three issues raised in his motion and, even if he had there is a triable dispute as to all three issues. As to whether Mr. Pettibon intended to gift the painting, the following evidence creates a triable dispute, even under heightened burden of proof: 1) Mr. Strike's testimony that Mr. Pettibon stated that he would give Mr. Strike the painting; 2) Mr. Pettibon did not receive a fee or commission for the painting; 3) Mr. Pettibon did not notify Regen Projects about the painting's existence upon its completion as he normally did,; 4) Ms. Regen and Ms. Overduin did not learn about the painting until 2007; and 5) Mr. Pettibon's purported 2009 statement that giving Mr. Strike the painting was like the time he gave his sister money and then regretted it. As to whether the painting was delivered to Mr. Strike, the following evidence creates a triable dispute: 1) all of the evidence mentioned previously as to the donative intent issue and 2) the painting was delivered to CAC based on arrangements made by Mr. Strike (United States v. Alcaraz-Gacia (9th. Cir. 1996) 79 F.3d 769, 775 (Delivery of a gift is complete when a donor delivers the gift to a third person acting on behalf of the donee, and the donor parts with dominion and control over the gift).) As to the issue whether the painting was consigned to Mr. Strike, the following evidence creates a triable dispute: Mr. Pettibon's fact 19 that he did not receive a fee or commission for the painting. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
